project.title: Kryptos K4 Research Dashboard
project.tagline: A grounded view of the repository's hypotheses, benchmarks, and latest structured runs.

benchmarks.label: Python CPU sweep
benchmarks.notes: Repository baseline for the pre-compiled Python heuristics.

benchmarks.label: CPU strategy runner
benchmarks.notes: Comparable fixture-first runner for the new corpus-backed K4 methods.

benchmarks.label: OpenCL GPU sweep
benchmarks.notes: Native OpenCL pass targeting an AMD Radeon 680M.

clues.label: K5 uses a similar coding system
K5 repeats BERLINCLOCK in the same position

strategy_catalog.name: Quagmire III Running Keys
strategy_catalog.objective: Test whether the solved K1-K3 plaintexts can act as direct running keys for K4.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 reuses the Kryptos mixed alphabet and one of the earlier panel plaintexts as its key material.

strategy_catalog.name: Spatial Matrix Reassembly
strategy_catalog.objective: Read padded 7x14 and 14x7 layouts in alternate orders to search for clue-bearing ciphertext runs.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 may hide anchor substrings through simple geometric rearrangement before substitution.

strategy_catalog.name: Hybrid Transposition Search
strategy_catalog.objective: Use hill climbing with clue overlap and local IoC heuristics to rank candidate column orders.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: A clue-bearing transposition may surface when column order improves both anchor overlap and local English-like structure.

strategy_catalog.name: Quagmire III Autokey
strategy_catalog.objective: Test theme-relevant primers under plain-autokey and cipher-autokey Quagmire variants.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 may bootstrap its own key stream from a short thematic primer.

strategy_catalog.name: Geometric Grilles
strategy_catalog.objective: Apply logical masks across a padded 7x14 grid to test simple grille-style extractions.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: A repeated mask pattern might expose the clue ciphertext as a contiguous extraction.

strategy_catalog.name: Chained Autokey
strategy_catalog.objective: Test double-layer Quagmire autokey combinations across theme-relevant primer pairs.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 could be a nested autokey construction requiring two sequential decryptions.

strategy_catalog.name: Segmented Resets
strategy_catalog.objective: Split K4 on 'W' and retry running-key and autokey decryption with per-segment resets.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: The repeated 'W' characters may mark segment boundaries or key resets.

strategy_catalog.name: Shifted Running Keys
strategy_catalog.objective: Exhaustively test all offsets of K1-K3 plaintexts and the KRYPTOS primer as running key material.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: The right offset into prior Kryptos text may align K4 with a standard running-key construction.

strategy_catalog.name: External Text Running Key
strategy_catalog.objective: Slide a historical text window across Howard Carter diary excerpts as candidate running keys.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: An external text tied to the K3 clue may supply the real running key stream for K4.

strategy_catalog.name: Fractionation Pipeline
strategy_catalog.objective: Expand Bifid-style candidates through repeating-key, autokey, and periodic-transposition post layers.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 may require fractionation plus at least one additional keyed layer before the anchor clues emerge.

strategy_catalog.name: Corpus Running Key
strategy_catalog.objective: Slide public corpus windows across K4 and rerank the strongest clue-aligned running-key decryptions.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: Public Howard Carter and official clue corpora contain usable key-stream fragments for a method-reconstruction track.

strategy_catalog.name: Periodic Transposition Hillclimb
strategy_catalog.objective: Search ragged periodic transposition families with keyword seeds and local permutation hillclimbs.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: K4 may be hiding its anchors inside a periodic transposition that needs stronger ranking than raw brute force.

strategy_catalog.name: Hybrid Pipeline Search
strategy_catalog.objective: Compose exactly two stages from fractionation, running-key, key-layer, and periodic-transposition families.
strategy_catalog.hypothesis: Sanborn's nested hints point to a short pipeline of interacting transforms rather than a single classical cipher.

results.name: Hybrid Pipeline Search
results.objective: Compose exactly two stages from fractionation, running-key, key-layer, and periodic-transposition families.
results.hypothesis: Sanborn's nested hints point to a short pipeline of interacting transforms rather than a single classical cipher.
results.summary: Top candidate scored 420/1000 via running_key:official:offset=10 -> periodic_transposition:w17:row->column.
notes: Local seed fan-out around running_key:official:offset=10 only.
Restricted periodic follow-through to widths 17, 19, and 28.
Seed set included identity, reversed identity, all default keyword permutations, reversed keyword permutations, and recycled recent local permutations.
Hillclimb rounds increased from 2 to 4 for this local search.

recommended_experiments.title: Cross-validate periodic transposition leads
recommended_experiments.thesis: Periodic structure keeps surfacing in strong candidates, but it still needs wider confirmation across standalone and hybrid searches.
notes: Periodic structure keeps surfacing in strong candidates, but it still needs wider confirmation across standalone and hybrid searches.
1 mapped strategies have not yet reinforced this family: 3.

recommended_experiments.title: Expand Bifid-led pipelines
recommended_experiments.thesis: Consensus candidates keep returning to Bifid-style seeds, so the next high-value work is deeper keyword and period validation plus downstream layering.
notes: Consensus candidates keep returning to Bifid-style seeds, so the next high-value work is deeper keyword and period validation plus downstream layering.

recommended_experiments.title: Cross-check running-key leads against new corpora
recommended_experiments.thesis: The ledger has enough running-key signal to justify explicit cross-document confirmation instead of treating it as a side branch.
notes: The ledger has enough running-key signal to justify explicit cross-document confirmation instead of treating it as a side branch.
3 mapped strategies have not yet reinforced this family: 11, 9, 8.
